steps.title: Combine the fractions on the right side
steps.content: The given equation is $$\frac{1}{q} = \frac{1}{p} + \frac{1}{r} + \frac{1}{s}.$$ Use the common denominator $prs$: $$\frac{1}{q} = \frac{rs}{prs} + \frac{ps}{prs} + \frac{pr}{prs} = \frac{pr + ps + rs}{prs}.$$

steps.title: Invert both sides to isolate q
steps.content: Taking the reciprocal of both sides gives $$q = \frac{prs}{pr + ps + rs}.$$

steps.title: Compare with the choices
steps.content:
• A) $p+r+s$: would only be right if $1/q$ equalled $1/(p+r+s)$, which it does not. Eliminated.
  • B) ${36}(p+r+s)$: the constant $36$ never appears in the equation. Eliminated.
  • C) $\frac{prs}{pr + ps + rs}$: matches the inverted form derived above.
  • D) $\frac{prs}{36p + 36r + 36s}$: introduces an unwarranted factor of $36$. Eliminated.
That confirms C is correct.
